message:
feat: add timeout and backpressure to PaneStepExecutor (ft-y9lnb.4)

- PaneStepExecutorConfig: per-step timeouts (default 30s for SendText),
  phase-level budget (sum of step timeouts + buffer), backpressure toggle
- FleetMemoryController integration: Emergency defers all steps, Critical
  defers non-pane steps, Elevated logs warning, Normal proceeds
- Phase-level timeout: tracks elapsed time across all steps, fails remaining
  steps when budget exceeded
- step_timeout_ms() / action_has_pane() helpers for clean dispatch
- 8 new tests (21 total PaneStepExecutor tests), all passing

Closes ft-y9lnb.4.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -115,7 +115,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-y9lnb.1","title":"Implement PaneStepExecutor: connect tx step execution to Robot Mode send/wait-for primitives","description":"# Goal
Create a PaneStepExecutor that implements the StepExecutor trait by dispatching step operations to actual panes via WeztermInterface.

# Critical Design Constraint: Sync Trait Inside Async Runtime
The StepExecutor trait is SYNC (returns Vec<T>). But the entire CLI runs inside rt.block_on(async { ... }) (main.rs line 16248). WeztermInterface methods are async (return Pin<Box<dyn Future>>). Calling block_on() inside an existing block_on() panics (nested runtime).

## Solution: Approach A (RECOMMENDED) — Make TxExecutionEngine async-capable
Add a parallel async trait:

    pub trait AsyncStepExecutor {
        async fn evaluate_gates(&self, contract: &MissionTxContract, now_ms: i64) -> Vec<TxPrepareGateInput>;
        async fn execute_steps(&self, contract: &MissionTxContract, fail_step: Option<&str>, now_ms: i64) -> Vec<TxCommitStepInput>;
        async fn execute_compensations(&self, commit_report: &TxCommitReport, fail_for_step: Option<&str>, now_ms: i64) -> Vec<TxCompensationStepInput>;
    }

Then either:
- Add TxExecutionEngine::execute_async() that takes AsyncStepExecutor, OR
- Make TxExecutionEngine generic over both sync and async executors via a unified trait with async fn

PaneStepExecutor implements AsyncStepExecutor. It can .await WeztermInterface calls directly since the CLI is already in an async context.

SyntheticStepExecutor and PolicyPrepareStepExecutor keep the sync trait. A blanket impl wraps sync executors into async ones.

## Alternative: Approach B — Use tokio::task::spawn_blocking
Call the sync StepExecutor methods from a blocking thread that CAN create a new runtime:
    let result = tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || {
        let rt = RuntimeBuilder::current_thread().build().unwrap();
        rt.block_on(async { handle.send_text(pane_id, &text).await })
    }).await;

This works but adds thread-hop overhead per step. Acceptable for the MVP.

## RECOMMENDATION
Start with Approach B (spawn_blocking) for the MVP — it's simpler and doesn't require changing TxExecutionEngine. Then migrate to Approach A in a follow-up bead if performance matters.

# Verified Types (plan.rs)
- StepAction enum (line 529): SendText { pane_id, text, paste_mode }, WaitFor { pane_id, condition, timeout_ms }, AcquireLock, ReleaseLock, StoreData, RunWorkflow, MarkEventHandled, ValidateApproval, NestedPlan, Custom
- TxStep { step_id: TxStepId, ordinal: usize, action: StepAction, description: String }
- TxCommitStepInput { step_id, success: bool, reason_code, error_code: Option, completed_at_ms }
- TxCompensation { for_step_id, action: StepAction }
- TxCompensationStepInput { for_step_id, success, reason_code, error_code: Option, completed_at_ms }

# WeztermInterface methods (verified)
- send_text(pane_id: u64, text: &str) -> WeztermFuture<()>
- send_text_with_options(pane_id, text, no_paste, no_newline) -> WeztermFuture<()>
- get_text(pane_id: u64, escapes: bool) -> WeztermFuture<String>
- list_panes() -> WeztermFuture<Vec<PaneInfo>>

# Design: PaneStepExecutor
## evaluate_gates(): delegate to PolicyPrepareStepExecutor (sync, no I/O, no nesting issue)

## execute_steps(): iterate contract.plan.steps, for each TxStep match on action:
- SendText { pane_id, text, paste_mode } -> spawn_blocking + block_on(handle.send_text(...))
- WaitFor { pane_id, condition, timeout_ms } -> spawn_blocking + block_on(poll get_text until match or timeout)
- StoreData { key, value } -> StorageHandle (sync, no nesting issue)
- AcquireLock/ReleaseLock -> PaneWorkflowLockManager if available
- Unsupported (RunWorkflow, NestedPlan, Custom, etc.) -> success: false, reason_code: "unsupported_action"
- On fail_step match: inject deterministic failure

## execute_compensations(): same dispatch but iterating contract.plan.compensations

# Unit Tests (12 minimum)
1. execute_steps SendText happy path: 3 steps, MockWezterm send_text succeed -> 3 success inputs
2. execute_steps SendText pane not found: send_text error -> failed input
3. execute_steps WaitFor match: get_text returns matching text -> success
4. execute_steps WaitFor timeout: pattern not found in time -> failed input
5. execute_steps StoreData: key/value stored -> success
6. execute_steps unsupported action: RunWorkflow -> failed with unsupported_action
7. execute_steps fail_step injection: forced failure at named step
8. execute_steps mixed: 3 steps, step 2 fails -> step 3 not executed
9. execute_compensations happy: compensation text sent -> success
10. execute_compensations failure: compensation send fails -> failed
11. execute_compensations empty: no compensations -> empty vec
12. evaluate_gates delegates: same results as PolicyPrepareStepExecutor

NOTE ON TESTS: Unit tests use MockWezterm which is NOT async — mock_wezterm_handle() returns synchronous mocks. Tests can use the simpler approach. The spawn_blocking path is only needed in the production code path where real WeztermInterface is genuinely async.

# Structured Logging
- tracing::info! per step: step_id, action variant, pane_id, duration_ms
- tracing::warn! on failure with error context
- tracing::instrument on execute_steps and execute_compensations

# Acceptance Criteria
1. PaneStepExecutor implements all 3 StepExecutor trait methods.
2. evaluate_gates delegates to PolicyPrepareStepExecutor.
3. execute_steps dispatches real WeztermInterface calls for SendText, WaitFor, StoreData.
4. Works when called from inside rt.block_on(async { ... }) (the CLI context).
5. All 12+ unit tests pass.
6. No unsafe code.
7. File: crates/frankenterm-core/src/tx_execution.rs (alongside existing executors).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:33.101908Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:50:14.230331Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T01:50:14.230100Z","close_reason":"Implemented PaneStepExecutor with 13 passing tests. Dispatches SendText, WaitFor, StoreData, AcquireLock, ReleaseLock, MarkEventHandled, ValidateApproval to real WeztermInterface. Failure boundary stops execution after first failed step. Uses thread::spawn + fresh runtime for sync->async bridge.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["executor","robot-mode","tx"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.1","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:33.101908Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-y9lnb.2","title":"Wire pre-commit gates to real Policy Engine: replace hardcoded true with policy.rs authorize() calls","description":"# Goal
Replace the hardcoded-true pre-commit gate inputs in plan.rs (lines 3016-3019) with real calls to the Policy Engine, pane registry, and approval token system.

# Background
The reality check found that mission_tx_prepare_gate_inputs() returns:
    policy_passed: true,
    approval_satisfied: true,
    target_liveness: true,
All three are hardcoded, meaning the prepare phase NEVER rejects a transaction. This defeats the entire safety guarantee that FrankenTerm promises.

The Policy Engine (policy.rs, 14,850 lines + 10 satellite modules) is fully implemented with:
- ActionKind enum with 26+ action types
- PolicyDecision (Allow/Deny/RequireApproval)
- Rate limiting, capability gates, secret redaction
- Approval token validation (approval.rs, 2,356 lines)

All the infrastructure exists — it just isn't wired.

# Design
## policy_passed gate
- For each step in the transaction, call PolicyEngine::authorize() with:
  - actor: the mission initiator identity
  - action: ActionKind matching the step type (SendText, ExecCommand, etc.)
  - target: the pane_id from the step assignment
- Aggregate: ALL steps must return Allow or RequireApproval. Any Deny → gate fails.

## approval_satisfied gate
- If any step returned RequireApproval, check the ApprovalTokenStore for valid tokens matching the (action, pane_id, fingerprint) tuple.
- Gate passes only when every RequireApproval step has a corresponding valid, unexpired approval token.
- Surface missing approvals in the prepare-phase output so operators know exactly what to approve.

## target_liveness gate
- For each unique pane_id in the transaction plan, check the pane registry (ingest.rs PaneRegistry or storage.rs panes table) for:
  - Pane exists in the current mux session
  - Pane was last seen within a configurable staleness threshold (default: 30s)
  - Pane is not in a known-dead state (exited, zombie)
- Gate fails if ANY target pane is missing or stale, with a specific list of which panes are problematic.

# Acceptance Criteria
1. A transaction targeting a pane where policy denies SendText is rejected at prepare phase with decision=Deny and specific step/policy reason.
2. A transaction requiring approval blocks at prepare phase with decision=RequireApproval and lists needed approval tokens.
3. A transaction targeting a dead/missing pane fails at prepare phase with liveness failure and specific pane IDs.
4. All three gates are individually testable with mock policy/registry.
5. Existing plan.rs tests that expect always-pass behavior are updated to use explicit policy allow-all config.
6. At least 12 new tests: 4 per gate covering pass, fail, partial, edge cases.
7. The gate evaluation produces observability events (TxObservabilityEvent) for each gate check result.

# Implementation Notes
- The gate function signature should accept a reference to PolicyEngine, ApprovalTokenStore, and PaneRegistry (or trait abstractions thereof).
- This is a pure logic change in plan.rs — no new files needed.
- The existing 123 plan.rs tests that assume gates pass should be adjusted to explicitly configure policy-allow-all rather than relying on hardcoded true.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SnowyHorse","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:55.327026Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-06T21:42:15.319483Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T21:42:15.319311Z","close_reason":"Already implemented by SnowyHorse. Real policy/approval/target gate traits with StorageBacked impls exist. PolicyPrepareStepExecutor wired in tx_execution.rs. Remote cargo check passed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gates","policy","safety","tx"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.2","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:55.327026Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2912,"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.2","author":"SnowyHorse","text":"Claiming ft-y9lnb.2 after closing wa-2dd4s.5. Next step: trace `mission_tx_prepare_gate_inputs()` in plan.rs and wire the hardcoded gate booleans to the real policy engine, approval path, and pane liveness sources before updating the existing tests.","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:59:31Z"},{"id":2913,"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.2","author":"SnowyHorse","text":"Implemented real tx prepare gates across plan.rs and tx_execution.rs. Policy authorization, approval-token scope checks, target liveness checks, and per-gate observability events are now wired; matching plan and execution tests were added. Remote rch validation is still running, and cargo check for frankenterm-core lib already passed.","created_at":"2026-04-06T19:47:19Z"},{"id":2914,"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.2","author":"SnowyHorse","text":"Implemented the real tx prepare-gate path in  and : prepare reports now carry per-step gate details, approval-blocked prepares have an explicit outcome, and the execution engine emits per-gate observability events. Added storage-backed approval/reservation/pane lookup adapters plus focused gate tests;  is green and the first remote test-binary build is in progress.","created_at":"2026-04-06T19:52:49Z"},{"id":2915,"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.2","author":"SnowyHorse","text":"Follow-up progress: fixed the CLI fallout from the new prepare outcome and repaired stale proptests/scenario expectations. Fresh rch runs are in flight for frankenterm-core test-target compile, workspace-wide cargo check, and cargo fmt check; fmt is green again.","created_at":"2026-04-06T19:55:34Z"},{"id":2916,"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.2","author":"SnowyHorse","text":"Additional validation pass in flight: I narrowed the fresh rch compile to the specific updated test targets (proptest_plan, proptest_plan_ext, tx_e2e_scenario_matrix, chaos_planner_dispatcher) so I can get faster signal on the stale shape fixes while the broader background checks continue.","created_at":"2026-04-06T20:04:50Z"},{"id":2917,"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.2","author":"SnowyHorse","text":"Fresh remote validation update: `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` passed via `rch` (remote exit 0). Follow-up remote runs are active for `cargo test -p frankenterm-core tx_prepare -- --test-threads=1`, `cargo clippy -p frankenterm-core --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` so I can separate bead-surface signal from unrelated workspace lint debt.","created_at":"2026-04-06T20:12:07Z"},{"id":2918,"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.2","author":"SnowyHorse","text":"Validation note: `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` failed remotely for an unrelated worker environment issue (`cairo-sys-rs` could not find `cairo.pc` on the worker; RCH-E307), not for bead-local compile errors. A scoped `cargo clippy -p frankenterm-core --all-targets -- -D warnings` attempt hit RCH dependency-preflight RCH-E324 and tried to fail open locally; I cancelled that path to respect the repo instruction that heavy cargo work must stay offloaded. The remote `cargo test -p frankenterm-core tx_prepare -- --test-threads=1` run is still active.","created_at":"2026-04-06T20:19:05Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-y9lnb.3","title":"Wire mission dispatch contracts to workflow engine for automated multi-pane dispatch","description":"# Goal
Connect MissionDispatchContract (plan.rs line 2593: { assignment_id: String, target_agent: String }) to the real workflow engine so mission loop decisions flow to actual agent dispatch.

# Background
mission_loop.rs has a real evaluation loop (safety envelope, retry storm detection, conflict detection, operator overrides) that produces MissionDispatchContract values. But these contracts are never acted upon. The WorkflowRunner can execute workflows. The gap is the mission -> workflow bridge.

# Design (single recommended approach)
## MissionStepWorkflow: Workflow trait impl that wraps PaneStepExecutor

1. Create a MissionStepWorkflow struct implementing the Workflow trait (workflows/traits.rs):
   - Takes a reference to PaneStepExecutor
   - When invoked, translates the detection event context into a StepAction (e.g., SendText to a target pane)
   - Executes via PaneStepExecutor (reusing the same execution path as ft tx run)
   - Returns WorkflowExecutionResult with the step outcome

2. Register MissionStepWorkflow with WorkflowRunner at startup:
   - WorkflowRunner::register_workflow(Arc::new(MissionStepWorkflow::new(executor)))
   - Use a rule_id prefix like "mission.dispatch.*" so find_matching_workflow returns it for mission events

3. Create a MissionDispatcher bridge (in mission_dispatch.rs or plan.rs):
   - fn dispatch(contracts: Vec<MissionDispatchContract>, runner: &WorkflowRunner, event_bus: &EventBus) -> Vec<DispatchResult>
   - For each contract: construct a synthetic Detection with rule_id = "mission.dispatch.<assignment_id>"
   - Call runner.handle_detection(&detection) -> WorkflowStartResult
   - Track invocation ID for coordination

4. Coordination:
   - Parallel contracts: dispatch all, collect WorkflowStartResults
   - Sequential contracts: await each WorkflowExecutionResult before dispatching next
   - Kill-switch: emit a MissionEventKind::KillSwitch event to event_bus, which WorkflowRunner can subscribe to for cancellation
   - Event bridging: MissionEventKind::Dispatched, ::DispatchCompleted, ::DispatchFailed

## Why this approach over alternatives
- Reuses existing WorkflowRunner infrastructure (concurrency limits, event bus integration, execution tracking)
- Reuses PaneStepExecutor (no pane operation logic duplication)
- Uses existing Workflow trait (no new trait needed)
- Mission dispatch is just another workflow trigger source, fitting the event-driven architecture

# Unit Tests (10 minimum)
1. Single contract dispatches one workflow -> success
2. Three parallel contracts -> three concurrent WorkflowStartResults
3. Sequential contracts -> order preserved
4. Contract for missing agent -> workflow fails, MissionEventKind::DispatchFailed emitted
5. Kill-switch during dispatch -> workflow cancellation attempted
6. Retry storm: 5 rapid failures -> mission loop retry detector fires (mock the count)
7. MissionEventKind::Dispatched includes assignment_id, target_agent, workflow_id
8. MissionEventKind::DispatchCompleted includes duration_ms and outcome
9. Empty contract list -> no workflows invoked, no events emitted
10. Dispatch with real PaneStepExecutor + MockWezterm -> send_text actually called on mock

# Structured Logging
- tracing::info! on dispatch: assignment_id, target_agent, workflow_id
- tracing::warn! on dispatch failure: error, assignment_id
- tracing::error! on kill-switch

# Acceptance Criteria
1. N dispatch contracts -> N workflow invocations via WorkflowRunner.
2. Sequential ordering respected when specified.
3. Kill-switch cancels active workflows.
4. Mission event log records dispatch metadata.
5. All 10+ tests pass.
6. No duplication of pane logic — delegates through PaneStepExecutor via MissionStepWorkflow.

# Implementation Notes
- WorkflowRunner::handle_detection() is the entry point for external triggers.
- Workflow trait (workflows/traits.rs) defines the interface.
- MissionDispatchContract is small (2 fields). The full step details come from MissionTxContract which the mission loop already has in scope.
- Consider adding a dispatch_contracts field to the mission evaluation result struct if it doesn't already exist, so the bridge knows what to dispatch.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:54:14.721996Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-06T23:08:22.470984Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dispatch","mission","tx","workflow"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.3","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:54:14.721996Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.3","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:55:02.595301Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"ft-y9lnb.4","title":"Add async execution wrapper with timeouts for real I/O in tx step execution","description":"# Goal
Add timeout enforcement and backpressure checks to PaneStepExecutor, handling the sync/async boundary where the StepExecutor trait is synchronous but WeztermInterface methods return futures.

# Critical Design Constraint: Sync Trait, Async I/O
The StepExecutor trait is synchronous (returns Vec<T>, not Future). But WeztermInterface methods are async (return WeztermFuture). PaneStepExecutor (ft-y9lnb.1) uses runtime_compat::block_on() to bridge this gap. Timeout enforcement wraps the block_on call:

    runtime_compat::block_on(async {
        runtime_compat::timeout(step_timeout, async {
            self.handle.send_text(pane_id, &text).await
        }).await
    })

This is the correct approach given the trait is sync. If the trait later becomes async (asupersync migration), this can be simplified.

# Design
## Timeout hierarchy
1. Step-level: from StepAction variant (WaitFor has explicit timeout_ms; SendText defaults to 30_000ms)
2. Phase-level: aggregate of step timeouts + 30s buffer (enforced by wrapping the entire execute_steps loop)
3. Transaction-level: from config or contract (not yet in MissionTxContract — pass via PaneStepExecutor config)

## Implementation approach
Build timeout enforcement directly into PaneStepExecutor rather than creating a decorator:
- Before each step: check elapsed time against phase budget
- Around each step's I/O: wrap in runtime_compat::timeout(step_timeout, ...)
- On timeout: build TxCommitStepInput { success: false, reason_code: "timeout", error_code: Some(format!("exceeded {timeout_ms}ms")) }

## Backpressure integration (verified API)
- FleetMemoryController::compound_tier() returns FleetPressureTier:
  - Normal — proceed
  - Elevated — proceed, log warning
  - Critical — defer non-critical steps (steps without pane_id), emit backpressure event
  - Emergency — defer ALL steps, return failed inputs for all remaining
- PaneStepExecutor optionally holds a reference to FleetMemoryController
- Check compound_tier() before each step in the execute_steps loop

## Unit Tests (10 minimum)
1. Step timeout: WaitFor with timeout_ms=100, mock pane returns text after 200ms -> timeout failure
2. Step within timeout: WaitFor with timeout_ms=5000, mock returns immediately -> success
3. Phase timeout: 3 steps, step 2 takes longer than phase budget -> step 2 timed out, step 3 skipped
4. No timeout specified: SendText uses default 30s, completes instantly -> success
5. Backpressure Normal: all steps proceed
6. Backpressure Elevated: all steps proceed, warning logged (verify tracing output)
7. Backpressure Critical: step without pane_id (StoreData) deferred, step with pane_id proceeds
8. Backpressure Emergency: all steps deferred, all inputs returned as failed with reason_code "backpressure_emergency"
9. Kill-switch (if applicable): transaction-level cancellation returns remaining steps as failed
10. Compensation with timeout: compensation step times out -> failed compensation input

## Structured Logging
- tracing::info! on timeout activation: step_id, timeout_ms, action variant
- tracing::warn! on backpressure deferral: pressure tier, step_id, action variant
- tracing::warn! on step timeout: step_id, elapsed_ms, timeout_ms
- tracing::error! on phase/transaction timeout: elapsed_ms, remaining_steps_count

# Acceptance Criteria
1. Step exceeding its timeout produces TxCommitStepInput with success=false, reason_code="timeout".
2. Phase-level timeout skips remaining steps.
3. Backpressure at Critical/Emergency defers appropriate steps.
4. All 10+ tests pass.
5. Structured tracing on all timeout and backpressure events.
6. No async trait change — works within the current sync StepExecutor interface.

# Implementation Notes
- Build into PaneStepExecutor (ft-y9lnb.1) rather than a separate decorator, since the sync/async boundary is already handled there.
- runtime_compat::timeout wraps the async block inside block_on.
- FleetMemoryController is optional — PaneStepExecutor works without it (all steps proceed).
- Keep this bead separate from ft-y9lnb.1 for clean dependency tracking: .1 creates PaneStepExecutor with basic dispatch, .4 adds timeout/backpressure on top.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:54:32.382728Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-06T23:07:52.962061Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["async","backpressure","timeout","tx"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.4","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:54:32.382728Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.4","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:55:02.817908Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- tracing::error! on phase/transaction timeout: elapsed_ms, remaining_steps_count

# Acceptance Criteria
1. Step exceeding its timeout produces TxCommitStepInput with success=false, reason_code="timeout".
2. Phase-level timeout skips remaining steps.
3. Backpressure at Critical/Emergency defers appropriate steps.
4. All 10+ tests pass.
5. Structured tracing on all timeout and backpressure events.
6. No async trait change — works within the current sync StepExecutor interface.

# Implementation Notes
- Build into PaneStepExecutor (ft-y9lnb.1) rather than a separate decorator, since the sync/async boundary is already handled there.
- runtime_compat::timeout wraps the async block inside block_on.
- FleetMemoryController is optional — PaneStepExecutor works without it (all steps proceed).
- Keep this bead separate from ft-y9lnb.1 for clean dependency tracking: .1 creates PaneStepExecutor with basic dispatch, .4 adds timeout/backpressure on top.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:54:32.382728Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:58:43.871995Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T01:58:43.871574Z","close_reason":"Added PaneStepExecutorConfig with per-step timeouts, phase-level timeout budget, and FleetMemoryController backpressure integration (Normal/Elevated/Critical/Emergency). 8 new tests (21 total PaneStepExecutor tests). Backpressure: Emergency defers all steps, Critical defers non-pane steps, Normal/Elevated proceed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["async","backpressure","timeout","tx"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.4","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:54:32.382728Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.4","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:55:02.817908Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-y9lnb.5","title":"Tx integration test suite: plan→compile→prepare(policy-gated)→commit(pane-targeted)→compensate end-to-end","description":"# Goal
Comprehensive integration test suite exercising the full tx pipeline via TxExecutionEngine with PaneStepExecutor, proving the data plane works end-to-end.

# Background
Today 316+ tx tests verify individual state machines and type serialization, mostly using SyntheticStepExecutor. With PaneStepExecutor (ft-y9lnb.1) and timeout support (ft-y9lnb.4), we can test the real execution path. The policy gate path is already real (ft-y9lnb.2 closed). This test suite proves the aggregate works: TxExecutionEngine<PaneStepExecutor>.execute() against MockWezterm panes.

# Design
## Test harness
- TxExecutionEngine<PaneStepExecutor> — the actual engine, not manual phase calls
- MockWezterm via mock_wezterm_handle() with add_default_pane(id) for each target pane
- MockPolicyAuthorizer, MockApprovalChecker, MockTargetLookup (plan.rs lines 7786-7853) for policy control
- Real in-memory IdempotencyStore for idempotent retry scenarios
- tracing_subscriber::fmt::TestWriter for structured log capture

## Test scenarios (18 total)

### Prepare Phase (4 tests)
1. Policy denial: MockPolicyAuthorizer returns Deny for pane 2 -> TxExecutionResult shows prepare denied
2. Approval required: MockApprovalChecker returns false -> prepare blocks with RequireApproval; then returns true -> prepare passes
3. Pane dead: MockTargetLookup returns None for pane 99 -> prepare fails with liveness error
4. All gates pass: clean prepare -> TxExecutionResult shows Prepared state

### Commit Phase (7 tests)
5. Happy path: 3-step tx, MockWezterm send_text all succeed -> TxExecutionResult shows Committed, 3 step results
6. Partial commit + compensation: 3 steps, step 2 targets missing pane -> step 1 compensated, step 3 skipped
7. Step timeout: WaitFor action with timeout_ms=50, mock doesn't deliver text -> timeout, compensation runs
8. Single-step tx: one SendText step, succeeds -> minimal path verified
9. Large tx: 50 steps -> proves bounded execution, all MockWezterm calls complete, resource cleanup
10. fail_step injection: --fail-step step-3 -> step 3 forced to fail, same behavior as SyntheticStepExecutor
11. Pane disappears mid-commit: MockWezterm returns pane for prepare, then errors on send at step 2

### Compensation Phase (3 tests)
12. Compensation succeeds: compensation action (SendText with "ROLLBACK") sent to mock pane -> success
13. Compensation failure: MockWezterm errors on compensation send -> TxExecutionResult shows compensation failed
14. No compensations defined: tx with no TxCompensation entries -> clean skip

### Cross-Cutting (4 tests)
15. Idempotent retry: run engine.execute() -> interrupt by marking step 1 as executed in IdempotencyStore -> re-run engine.execute() -> step 1 skipped, step 2 executed
16. Kill-switch: contract with kill_switch=SafeMode -> prepare gates include kill-switch check
17. Backpressure: configure FleetMemoryController at Critical -> non-critical steps deferred
18. Observability: verify engine.execute() produces TxObservabilityEvent sequence: PrepareStarted -> PreconditionValidated -> CommitStarted -> StepCommitted (x3) -> CommitCompleted for a 3-step happy path

## Logging
- tracing_subscriber TestWriter captures structured logs per test
- Each test on failure prints: execution trace, policy decisions, mock pane calls, idempotency ledger state
- Assert on both TxExecutionResult AND observability events

# Acceptance Criteria
1. All 18 tests pass.
2. Tests use TxExecutionEngine<PaneStepExecutor>, not manual phase calls.
3. Tests run in <15 seconds (mock panes, no real I/O).
4. Every test asserts on TxExecutionResult + observability events.
5. Test file: crates/frankenterm-core/tests/tx_integration_suite.rs
6. On failure, structured logs show exactly which step failed and why.
7. MockWezterm configured with add_default_pane(id) for target panes.

# Implementation Notes
- Depends on ft-y9lnb.1 (PaneStepExecutor) and ft-y9lnb.4 (timeout support).
- ft-y9lnb.2 (policy gates) is closed — real PolicyPrepareStepExecutor exists.
- Mock infrastructure in plan.rs test module (lines 7786+): MockPolicyAuthorizer, MockApprovalChecker, MockTargetLookup. May need pub(crate) re-export for use from tests/ directory.
- Use #[cfg(feature = "subprocess-bridge")] if tx types require this gate.
- IdempotencyStore is in tx_idempotency.rs — construct in-memory for tests.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":300,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:54:56.409566Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T00:50:07.571352Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","integration","testing","tx"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.5","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:54:56.409566Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.5","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:55:03.016750Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.5","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:55:03.225319Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.5","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:55:03.440846Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-y9lnb.6","title":"Wire ft tx run CLI command to use PaneStepExecutor for real pane operations","description":"# Goal
Wire both human CLI (ft tx run/rollback) and Robot Mode (ft robot tx run/rollback) to use TxExecutionEngine<PaneStepExecutor> for real pane operations.

# Background (verified from main.rs)
Both CLI paths use synthetic helpers imported at line 24:
  tx_prepare_gate_inputs_allow_all as build_robot_tx_prepare_gate_inputs
This bypasses all policy gates. The CLI also doesn't use TxExecutionEngine — it manually calls phase functions.

# Output Type Mapping (verified)
TxExecutionResult (from TxExecutionEngine::execute()) has:
  final_state, outcome, prepare_report, commit_report, compensation_report, events, ledger, forensic_bundle

RobotTxRunData (CLI output struct, main.rs line 5438) has:
  contract_file, tx_id, plan_id, prepare_report, commit_report, compensation_report, final_state

Mapping: construct RobotTxRunData from TxExecutionResult + contract metadata:
  RobotTxRunData {
      contract_file: contract_path.display().to_string(),
      tx_id: contract.intent.tx_id.0.clone(),
      plan_id: contract.plan.plan_id.0.clone(),
      prepare_report: result.prepare_report,
      commit_report: result.commit_report,
      compensation_report: result.compensation_report,
      final_state: result.final_state,
  }

# Design (Approach B: refactor to TxExecutionEngine)

## ft tx run handler (line 36138)
Replace ~180 lines of manual orchestration with:
1. Construct PaneStepExecutor (needs WeztermHandle, StorageHandle, PolicyEngine)
2. let engine = TxExecutionEngine::new(executor, config);
3. let result = engine.execute(&mut contract, now_ms)?;
4. Construct RobotTxRunData from result (mapping above)
5. Emit via emit_mission_success()

Fallback: if WeztermHandle unavailable (no active watcher), use SyntheticStepExecutor with warning.

## ft tx rollback handler (line 36321)
Same pattern: construct engine, run compensation phase.

## ft robot tx run/rollback (lines 20599, 20773)
Same refactor as human CLI but output via print_robot_response().

## --dry-run flag (new)
Add --dry-run to TxCommands::Run:
- Runs prepare phase only (real policy gates via PolicyPrepareStepExecutor)
- Prints step plan without executing commit: step IDs, target panes, action summaries
- Returns structured output showing what WOULD happen
- No pane operations, no compensation

## Changes to imports (line 24)
Remove: tx_prepare_gate_inputs_allow_all as build_robot_tx_prepare_gate_inputs
Remove: mission_tx_commit_step_inputs as build_robot_tx_commit_step_inputs
Remove: mission_tx_compensation_inputs as build_robot_tx_compensation_inputs
These are no longer needed when using TxExecutionEngine.

# Unit Tests (6 minimum)
1. ft tx run: engine.execute() called, RobotTxRunData constructed correctly from result
2. ft tx run --fail-step: failure injection works through engine
3. ft tx run --dry-run: only prepare phase runs, no commit, output shows plan
4. ft tx rollback: compensation runs through engine
5. ft robot tx run: same as #1 but verify JSON envelope format
6. ft tx run without WeztermHandle: falls back to SyntheticStepExecutor with warning message

# Structured Logging
- tracing::info! CLI entry: contract_file, executor_type (pane vs synthetic fallback), dry_run, fail_step
- tracing::info! completion: outcome, final_state, elapsed_ms

# Acceptance Criteria
1. Both ft tx run and ft robot tx run use TxExecutionEngine<PaneStepExecutor>.
2. ft tx rollback and ft robot tx rollback use PaneStepExecutor for real compensation.
3. No remaining import of tx_prepare_gate_inputs_allow_all in the CLI binary.
4. --dry-run validates permissions without executing commit.
5. All existing flags (--fail-step, --paused, --kill-switch, --format) preserved.
6. Fallback to SyntheticStepExecutor when no watcher running.
7. All 6+ tests pass.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-04-06T21:44:28.835835Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:04:53.413443Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","tx","wiring"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.6","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:44:28.835835Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.6","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:44:32.410453Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.6","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:44:32.622105Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-01x","title":"Version compatibility check: detect incompatible wa/database combinations","description":"# Task: Version Compatibility Check

## Goal
Prevent users from accidentally running incompatible wa versions against their database.

## Scenarios
1. **Newer wa, older DB**: Migration needed (usually safe)
2. **Older wa, newer DB**: DANGEROUS - wa cannot read newer schema
3. **Major version jump**: May require manual intervention

## Implementation

### Database Version Tracking
Track schema_version, min_compatible_wa, created_by_wa, created_at in wa_meta table.

### Startup Check
On open, verify:
- current_wa >= min_compatible_wa (else: WaTooOld error)
- schema_version <= supported_schema (else: SchemaTooNew error)
- schema_version == supported_schema (else: MigrationNeeded)

### User-Facing Messages
Old wa + new DB: Clear error with upgrade instructions
New wa + old DB: Offer migration with backup

## Testing
- Unit tests: version comparison logic
- Integration: simulate version mismatches
- E2E: upgrade/downgrade scenarios

## Acceptance Criteria
- Old wa refuses to open newer DB with clear error
- New wa offers migration for older DB
- Version info tracked in database metadata
- Helpful upgrade instructions provided","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:58:00.500288986Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:41:55.706395892Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T17:41:55.7058907Z","close_reason":"Added wa_meta version compatibility checks + schema gating","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-2vr1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-mxzp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.11","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-rnf.5","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"}]}
